g.data-narration: Welcome to Artificial Intelligence - a branch of computer science creating systems that perform human-intelligent tasks.
AI creates systems that perform tasks requiring human intelligence like learning, reasoning, and problem-solving.
g.text: Artificial Intelligence is a branch of computer science that aims to create systems
capable of performing tasks that typically require human intelligence.
Key Tasks: Learning | Reasoning | Problem-solving | Perception | Language Understanding

g.data-narration: There are four fundamental approaches to AI: thinking humanly, thinking rationally, acting humanly, and acting rationally.
The thinking humanly approach uses cognitive modeling to understand and replicate human thought processes through psychology.
g.text: Based on cognitive science and experimental psychology.
Goal: Understand and replicate human thought processes.

g.data-narration: The thinking rationally approach uses formal logic and mathematical reasoning, exemplified by expert systems using predicate logic.
g.text: Based on formal logic and mathematical reasoning.
Uses propositional and predicate logic for inference.

g.data-narration: Acting humanly requires passing the Turing Test using NLP, knowledge representation, automated reasoning, and machine learning.
g.text: AI systems that act like humans, capable of passing
the Turing Test. Requires multiple capabilities:

g.data-narration: Acting rationally is the dominant paradigm where a rational agent perceives environment and takes actions to maximize performance.
g.text: Dominant paradigm in modern AI. A rational agent
perceives environment and takes actions to maximize

g.data-narration: AI systems have core components: perception, knowledge representation, reasoning, learning, and action for decision making.
Knowledge representation methods include semantic networks, frames, production rules, and ontologies for structuring information.
Three main reasoning types: deductive from general to specific, inductive from specific to general, and abductive for best explanation.
Three learning types: supervised uses labeled data, unsupervised finds patterns in unlabeled data, and reinforcement learns from rewards.
AI by capability: Narrow AI for specific tasks, General AI for human-level tasks, and Super AI surpassing human intelligence.
AI by functionality: reactive machines, limited memory, theory of mind, and self-aware AI - progressing in sophistication.
Real-world AI applications span healthcare for diagnosis, finance for fraud detection, transportation for autonomous vehicles, and manufacturing.
Key exam points: four AI approaches, rational agent as dominant paradigm, core components, classifications, and real-world applications.
g.text: 1. Four approaches: Thinking Humanly, Thinking Rationally, Acting Humanly (Turing Test), Acting Rationally (Rational Agent)
2. Rational Agent approach is the dominant paradigm in modern AI - systems maximize performance measure
3. Core components: Knowledge Representation, Reasoning, Learning, Perception
4. Current AI is predominantly Narrow/Weak AI - General and Super AI remain hypothetical
5. Know real-world applications: Healthcare, Finance, Transportation, Manufacturing

g.data-narration: AI computational complexity: search problems are O(b^d), constraint satisfaction is NP-complete, planning is PSPACE-complete.
In summary, AI creates systems performing human-intelligent tasks using the rational agent approach as the dominant modern paradigm.
g.text: AI aims to create systems that perform tasks requiring human intelligence
The rational agent approach (acting rationally) is the dominant modern paradigm
Focus on: Four approaches, core components, AI classifications, real-world applications
